text: I really, really love this stuff! I've always had bad feet, but had never lived in such a dry climate as Nevada. My fingernails all cracked and tore within a couple of weeks of moving here late last summer, and my feet weren't much better. Enter winter. My hands improved with regular trips for a manicure and frequent application of hand lotion, but only this Gold Bond Pedi Smooth cream has helped my feet. I lather it on every night before bed and apply an "intensive" form of a Nivea lotion before putting on socks in the morning. My feet are great now, and my manicurist agrees. The Gold Bond was OK on my hands, but it left a weird kind of stickiness on them and I stopped using it for hands. For feet, though, you can't beat it.

text: I love the BeautyWill Professional Makeup Brush set, It arrived a week ahead of scheduled. It comes in a resealable package which is a plus. So if you are looking for a set to use with both liquid and dry foundation or if you need to apply glitter or face paint I think this set would be a good match for that. Hope that my review has helped someone make there decision and if you have an questions ask away lol.

text:
This face masque is both hydrating and gently brightening. I have used this on my face daily for 30 minutes for the past 2-3 weeks, and have a seen great results. My skin is nourished without being greasy, my skin tone evens out after use, and it has slowly started to fade my acne scarring. This masque is so gentle, that I also use it on my eyelids and around my eyes. It's natural, so it doesn't irritate that sensitive skin, but gives both my eyes and face the moisture it needs.

Would definitely recommend--especially to people with dry skin and acne. It's a great thing to add into your regime, and even though you may not feel it tingling or making your skin super tight (which is how I typically knew my past masques were 'working'), it really is effective.

And lastly, this is a steal! There is so much product in the container, and you only need about the size of a quarter every time you use it. Definitely get your money's worth.

text:
I am reviewing this on behalf of my boyfriend who is the lucky user of this electric shaver. He still insists that razor a blade gives a closer shave and I agree, the skin is a lot smoother and clearer but this is great for the weekends when he wants to get rid of the stubble and not scratch my face off. This enables him to get a smooth feeling on the skin without stripping and irritating it, and no creams and products are needed. The 3 rotating heads really adjust to the contour of the face and they snap open for easy clean up with water. I've seen him use it and it's a cinch, fast, easy and no mess!

Overall this is a great product, it works well and the face feels and looks good afterwards and it takes very little time to use. Better than shaving when it comes to comfort but my man still prefers the razor when he has to shave for work. This makes a great holiday gift, a gadget that people wouldn't run out and buy for themselves necessarily and fun to have as an option in the morning.

- Kasia S.

text:
I am a self imposed victim of over plucking my brows, and of my lashes seeming to become shorter and more sparse as I'm aging.

I've been wanting to try some of the many lash growth serums for a long time. The first thing I tried was Castor Oil. It seemed to help my lashes grow, and look a bit thicker. I then got this LashRevive serum, and another serum at the same time. I used LashRevive on my right lashes and brow, and the other brand on the left lashes and brow. I will only be discussing results for the right eye, with the LashRevive serum in this review.

I noticed a lot of wonderful reviews for this product, lots of pictures of the bottle, but no before and after pictures.
I received my product on the 11th of June, and today is June 25th, so I have been using this product consistently for 2 weeks.

First for the lashes. I am including a picture of before and after so you can judge for yourselves. The left side is before, right side is after. I really see little difference. When I look in the mirror, I feel as though they are more noticeable, maybe a tiny bit thicker, or darker, but not by much. But as you can see, the picture reflects none of that. Maybe it's just wishful thinking. I can say that on my lower lashes, mostly toward the inner corner, I have seen growth. They are much longer. It's weird that they are only on the inside though. I do not have before/after pictures of that, as I was not expecting this to effect the lower lashes.

On to the brows. I can see a difference in my brow. It appears fuller to me. I'm a natural blonde, so the newest hairs that are growing in are very light, almost white blonde, and difficult to see in the included picture, but I do believe you can see a tiny bit of a difference. Nothing dramatic. It's enough for me to continue to use it for my brows. Again, before picture on the left, after on the right. Judge for yourselves.

Application was super easy. It's a thick, milky consistency, and has a nice large applicator brush to easily cover the lashes and brow area quickly. I experienced no irritation to the skin, or to the eye. Never felt any sort of sting or burn at all. I did have gunk in the corners of my eye in the morning, and weird white stringy stuff in my lashes. Easy enough to remove when I washed my face in the morning. I did notice what seemed like more lashes than normal actually fall out, in the beginning. Maybe it was only because I was paying more attention to my lashes though. It was about 2-3 per day. Obviously not enough to be noticeable as you can see in the before/after pic.

I'm confused with this product. I did not see enough results to give this 4 or 5 stars. But saw enough of a result on the brow to think (hope) that I may get better results with continued use, so I did not give it just 1 star. I am going to continue to use this product until I run out, and I will update this review with any positive/negative results.

I received this product for free in exchange for my review. I hope this was helpful for you.

text: I first learned about this sunscreen from No BS Beauty (a no-nonsense, non-sponsored skincare enthusiast on YouTube). I was looking for something with good staying power, good filters (beyond FDA archaic limits), and no scent/fragrance for both everyday and outdoor activity sun exposure.  This hits all the marks. I have maturing skin (almost 50), and use tret, so UV exposure protection is a must. I've repurchased this one throughout the year (2022). It has never failed me: long, multi-hour summer hikes, summer outdoor wedding, and now, just as an SPF make-up primer for everyday life (work, errands, etc). I would say it does leave a slight white cast on my medium-olive skin tone, but that disappears with my sheer BB cream or foundation. It leaves a not-quite matte, smooth/silky, almost powdery finish that I appreciate because, having maturing combo-skin, I cannot stand all those highly praised "dewy" sunscreens that make me look greasy moreso than "dewy."  If you're seeking "dewy," this isn't it. I wouldn't say it's matte or drying either -- it just hits the sweet spot for me. I am a bit of a skincare nerd who wants to try out lots of different sunscreens, and I have no doubt there are amazing alternatives out there, but when you find something that works? At least in my case, I end the hunt and just hit re-order. Make sure you check the seller's storefront and ratings (always recommend that when purchasing J or Kbeauty skincare products from Amazon). This one is definitely worth trying and adding to your collection!

text:
This Kardashian dry black seed oil is by far the best shining/straightening oil I have ever used in my life. I am on 2 different types of chemo drugs and it has played havoc on my long hair. Kim Kardashian knows her stuff for sure! I have tried at least 30 different products to help with the frizz in my hair. I received a small sample bottle of this in a monthly subscription box I get. A small bottle of this lasted me about 4 months of daily use. I used it right after a wash while my hair was still wet and then right after blow drying and using the straightening iron and then a tiny bit to give my hair that shiny finished, sleek look. Let me tell you, my hair looks and feels like silk after using this. First off, the tangles fall right out. The heat from my iron doesn't damage my hair any longer and my husband says my hair feels like spun silk! This is also a dry oil so there is no oily build up or feel at all. Also, look at the AMAZING price that this is offered at! Wow! I will be heartbroken if they ever stop selling this stuff!
Now, there is only one con to this dry black seed oil. It has a small aroma that to me smells like pencil shavings. Almost like when you were in elementary school standing at the pencil sharpener getting your pencils all sharpened up for your tests! The good news is that the smell doesn't stay in your hair but for a few minutes. Other than that, you can't go wrong with this product!

text:
Stretch marks occur when skin is abnormally stretched and expanded for a period of time. The abnormal stretching causes the skin's support structure of collagen and elastin to break down or rupture. The visible stretch marks on the skin are actually bands of elastin that have broken inside and out of the skin. The skin can not get back to its original form quickly.

Stretch marks are among the toughest skin-care concerns to treat ... No matter how good a product is, change takes time ... you can't see results immediately. It takes commitment to apply stretch mark treatments two to three times daily over several months.

As for this cream, it is smooth to apply and is absorbed quickly by the skin. It is not greasy either.
I am losing weight and I can see a few silver lines on my inner arms. I rub a small amount of the product on my silver stretch marks in circular motions twice a day, once in the morning and once before bed. After a week they are visible only if I look for them now. This cream does lighten the mark to negligible and excellent for preventing further stretch marks.

I received this product in exchange for a fair and honest review!!

Any women who has heard anything about skin care has heard of Retinol. The properties of Retinol helps plump up the skin while sloughing off the dead dulling skin cells and revealing the fresh glowing skin underneath. I have tried my fair share of cosmetics and skin care products and I have to say if a Retinol can be applied as a healing agent and moisturizer, you have hit the jackpot. I didn't quite feel the "working tingle" as I had with other products but loved the moisturizing properties of this cream. I applied mine to a clean face and then applied my makeup over it. It made my skin feel great! The built in pump dispenser concerned me that I would be leaving product in the jar so I was pleased to find out that I can easily remove the screw on lid to access every last drop.
I was offered this product at a reduced price for my unbiased and honest opinion.

text:
With summer here, I always let my little girls have colored hair. I bought this Kanekalon braiding hair for my youngest daughter to join in the fun.
The Kanekalon hair is really good quality. You will get three bundles of 24 inch ombré blue to purple hair. The hair has some wave texture to it, making it very easy to separate and braid into actual hair. I also love to use this to make dreads. Takes little effort with steam or heat to seal it. I was even able to straighten it with my hair straightener on the lowest setting without it melting.
I love the bright color. Washes very well, no bleeding or color loss. I would recommend this to others. My daughter had a ML pony themed birthday party and I enough hair came in this bundle that I was able to make 12 braids for her friends to wear.

text:
I really like this bronzer, and it's hard to find in stores.
I've been using it since I was 40 (I used to use the clear red, but swapped over to the bronzer when I turned 50).
For an older woman it's much nicer than a heavy foundation and fake-red-clown-cheeks. It provides the correct amount of color and allows us to keep our face out of the sun. For a younger woman you'll get that "oh, you look so beautiful with no makeup" comment all the time. It's a lovely translucence.

As a result, at 60 I have no wrinkles, but maintain a healthy glow. (Of course I get tons of exercise and drink lots of water also)
This is a terrific product for anyone between the ages of 18 and 70.

text:
I didn't think the unit was working, it has vague instructions, but just touch it to dry bare skin and you'll feel it. I did not come wit a AC plug, (minus one star for that), you'll have to use one from another gadget, I used my tablet one. When it is charged, press and hold the button in until it beeps and lights up. There are two levels of power, I think, press the button one more time, the indicator light on the power button is orange, press it one more time and you'll see red. It runs for 3 minutes and then shuts off. You can use it a total of 30 minutes in one day. Diagrams in the booklet show you which way to use it effectively. This is important- you need to use a hydro gel or a oil, like rose hip oil or such like I do or you will feel a slight electric shock against your skin.
Have I seen improvements? YES. I had a NUface facial and this gadget seems to being doing as well as the NUface mini. I use it every day and I notice tighter skin, less redness, and less wrinkles. I use it just 3 minutes every morning. For the price I think it is a nice portable massage. I use it also with my cellulite cream on my legs and without changing anything else, the cellulite is fading (been only a week).

text:
I always wondered how you can see some guys always sporting a three-day beard. It seems with my beard it is either too short or way too long. This product provides an excellent choice of three beard trimming guides (guides reflect the following numbers on the guides -- 1/2 and a 1 and a 1-1/2). You may want to consider starting with the highest number and working your way to the smallest to make sure you don't do what I did and make your beard immediately shorter than you anticipated. However, with that said, I found the perfect combination for me is using the 1/2 which is the equivalent of a three-day beard.

When receiving the product it shipped with a travel guard for safety. With no guard, you could easily shave your head. The quality of the product is superb with excellent weight and is easy to use. On/off switch is an easy up for on and down for off. A green light comes on to let you know that you are in business. It is a quieter device than most. I charged the battery when it first arrived using the provided USB cord and it is still going strong. NOTE. You must have an AC adapter that you can plug into the wall. This device does not come with it. However, you can charge on your computer or other charging device.

I love it! You will not be disappointed.

text:
That's the only "negative" thing I have to say about this shaver. I razor my head to a smooth finish and was hoping this shaver would do the trick. Unfortunately it doesn't get it smooth like a razor but it comes close, shorter than peach fuzz.
It holds a charge for a good long time, two weeks of me using it every other day. Keep in mind, that made using it quick, like two minutes or less to get around my head and face.
The shape makes it comfortable to hold and it conforms to the head nicely, gliding over the surface with ease. I am able to easily get around the tops of my ears without difficulty and it is quite gentle on the skin. Every once in a while I can feel it tug on my neck but that is rare. Going over my head, i don't feel a thing.
I only use it in the shower so as soon as I'm done, I pop open the heads and rinse it out. Super easy.
I've only used a couple of the attachments, namely the ear/nose trimmer and the base trimmer. Both work as they should.
Good waterproof shaver and a really good price. I'm glad I purchased this and hope to get a lot of use from it.

text:
This eye cream was far different than I was expecting -- but in a GREAT way. I'm 29-years-old, so the lines around my eyes aren't terribly deep, although they are probably deeper than many other females my age due to my sun-worshiping in earlier years. I do suffer, however, from severe seasonal allergies and am constantly plagued with dark circles ("allergic shiners" as they call them) and extreme puffiness of both my upper eyelids and just under my eyes (the infamous "bags").

To be honest, my impression of this cream at first glance wasn't too great. For starters, the jar of cream was much smaller than I had imagined, but I guess that's my fault for not noticing the (very) small-printed "0.5 fl oz" on the label in the picture. (I do think that this was a little deceitful though, because the size/amount of this cream is not listed anywhere on the product description.) In addition, while trying to read the teeny tiny print on the jar, I also almost immediately noticed a spelling typo on the front of the label that reads "Nautral & Healthy" rather than "Natural & Healthy." I know this seems like a rather minuscule and knit-picky detail to point out, but it did make me at least wonder about this company's quality control standards.

Despite my initial impression, I gave the company/cream the benefit of the doubt and have been using it for the last few days. With my first application under my eyes, the cream felt more on the "thin" side (similar to a lotion rather than thick cream), but while rubbing the cream into my skin in a circular motion, the moisture that the cream delivered was far better than I anticipated. It takes at least 3-4 minutes before your skin feels like the cream is completely absorbed, which gave me ample time to slowly massage the area during absorption (and felt very soothing and relaxing). I had absolutely no issues with any side effects or skin reactions such as burning, tingling, redness, or irritation. I noticed a small "immediate" reduction in the pores and fine lines of the areas I applied the cream, though not as impressive as some of the Retinol and Vitamin C serums that I've used in the past. There was no obvious difference in the dark circles or puffiness right after application, however, within about 30-45min, the puffiness started to visibly decrease and the area all around my eyes felt less tender/achy in general.

With each subsequent application, I noticed the same resulting decrease in puffiness as was observed initially. My puffiness did return each morning, but I expected because I'm still constantly being exposed to my allergens. (After all, the cream is meant to relieve a symptom of my allergies, not be a miracle cure for my chronic systemic condition.) I've noticed that if I apply the cream immediately before bed and spend more time rubbing it into my skin in the circular motions, I do have a noticeable decrease in the morning when I wake up compared to before I started using the cream. As far as the under-eye dark circles, I can't tell a difference so far - but in all fairness, I've only been using the cream for 4 days (twice daily) so there's still hope.

Now for the best part (in my opinion, at least). On the second night of using this cream, I had yet another one of my throbbing headaches (allergies are the worst!!) About 15-20min after my evening cream application, I noticed that my headache felt a little better -- I'm not saying that there was some miraculous change here, but it did feel less severe. This was very unusual for me, as I hadn't taken any ibuprofen or Zyrtec at that point (my usual go-to medications when my headaches are severe). The only thing I could think that I had done differently this time than any other time was that I applied the cream -- it was a strange connection, but stranger things have certainly happened to me. I went back to look at the ingredients listed on the jar and saw that it had rooibos in it. I had absolutely no idea what this was (and probably didn't notice it the first time I looked because I don't have a magnifying glass), but I looked it up online and read that it's an herb from South Africa with a laundry-list of health benefits -- including antioxidant properties and... headache relief?!?! Although still relatively skeptical that this was just a coincidence and that I had gotten "lucky" on my google search, I was still optimistic and proceeded to rub the eye cream on my temples and at the base of my skull to see what would happen. After a good 30-45min, my headache was almost completely gone. I know this headache thing sounds ridiculous and obscure, but I encourage anyone to research this mysterious Rooibos for themselves. I haven't had a headache as severe as that one since, but I have had a few headaches start to come on, and with each headache, I applied the cream to my temples, under my eyes, and to the base of my skull and the feeling has gone away each time within about an hour.

With all of this said, I couldn't rate this "eye" cream turned headache remedy anything less than 5 stars despite my initial less-than-thrilled first impression. Because the cream is light and only requires a very small amount with each application, I think the small jar will actually last me a reasonably long time, too. Between the headache relief and the disappearing eye bags, I would be confident in recommending this product to others. I was also fortunate to receive my first jar of this cream at a discounted price or free in exchange for my honest and unbiased review.

text: This USpicy Ion Hair Straightener Brush came extremely well packaged. I did not personally test this unit on my hair but watched my friend use and demonstrate its capabilities. It comes with the brush, storage pouch. a couple hair clips, heat glove and user guide.The Bush has a very solid salon quality build. The unit also has a safety Auto Off which shuts the unit down after 30 min of nonuse which I believe is essential. We found this unit to be fully useable in only 30 seconds of heating due to the MCH Heating Technology. The adjustable temperature is displayed by LED on the handle which is initially set at 350 degrees and adjustable up and down from there. The actual results for straightening were amazing to both of us. Obviously results are going to vary depending on your hair. Most people use the so called straightening wand whereas this is an actual brush that works phenomenal. The bristles are solid and do not move like a traditional bristled brush which allows the heat to transmit through the hair without burning scalding or frizzing. Guys out there - if your lady likes to straighten her hair - buy this for her! You won't be disappointed.

text:
I was using a cheaper model rotary style brush for the previous 2 years when it finally broke. They used a thin metal that reached all the way up into the brush. It rusted and snapped. I pulled it apart and found it was about as thick as a the metal in a small paperclip. Braun uses a much thicker metal stem system that does not go all the way up to the brush, but rather the brush is made to reach down to the stem. Much better build quality. Much easier to dry off if you want to and being at least 3 times thicker it will last for years compared to the other brush.
When I researched the heck out of Electric brushes, I found the Braun 3D Excel was #1. Yes, it topped the famous sonicare. Sonicare does a better job advertising while I spent less $$ and have a better product doing what it should be doing. Cleaning my teeth. I do tell people about this product. Its something that actually lives up to its repuation. Its one of the great products in my opinion. After about a week of using mine, I ordered another and had it sent to my mom.
When I first turned it on, I was impressed already. I could tell it was high quality. Then I began using everyday and have done so for over 1 month now. I love the timer and try to brush 2x a day as recommended. Its great and my teeth have improved. I can tell they are smoother and whiter and feel much better. I can feel that my gums and teeth have tightened a little better where I used to feel bigger gaps when flossing. I will never go back to using a stick brush and I can only imagine how impressive the next generation of electric toothbrushes will be. Awesome already and if they improve on this, it will be amazing. If youre too cheap for Amazon, they can be found on ebay too. Its worth the money especially when it keeps the dentist away. I rate this product 5 huge STARS. Get it and you wont regret it.

text:
[[VIDEOID:f7a496b8573ef75cce856e43697157ad]] What I do like:
1. The color is vibrant.
2. It’s very cute and does give the oversized look when wearing it.

What I don’t like and why I didn’t give 5 stars:
1. It gets “runs” easily. You can see from my picture there’s several “runs” like would happen to a set of tights. When you’re wearing it however they’re not obvious at all.
2. This might be a no brainer because of the size but this is not really a wrist scrunchie, it’s so large wrist wear isn’t practical. You’ll need to either commit to wearing this all day or have some bag or purse to keep it in, this is slightly less practical for daily use then I personally like.
3. It’s not tight enough to be used alone, you’ll need to first use a regular hair tie and then add this for decoration, I prefer scrunchies alone because hair ties hurt after a while but this scrunchie is too slick to stay by itself and would need to be folded over several times eliminating that cute chunky oversized look that this is supposed to give.

Although I’m a little let down by the practical use potential of the product, I do like this and will definitely continue to use it for special occasions just not daily wear.

The first edition of The Presence came out in 1945 and went through two printings. Berthold von Schenk had by then been a LCMS parish pastor for 27 years and was serving his third parish, Our Saviour Lutheran Church in the Bronx, New York. The material came from years of sermons, lectures and retreat meditations. The Presence is his confession of what the Holy Eucharist means to him and what it can mean for us: the actual presence and self-giving of the living Christ.

The 65 years since its original publication have not diminished the power of The Presence to illuminate the well-known narratives and doctrines of our faith in striking ways. It would be a rare pastor or layperson who reads it through without recognizing thereafter holy ground not seen before in worship and life, and without an ever-deepening love for Holy Communion.
This edition is an updated printing of von Schenk's original 1945 edition. Footnotes and an introduction are provided by The Rev. Paul Robert Sauer, current pastor of Our Saviour Lutheran Church in the Bronx, New York.

text:
Short summary: I like a number of the instruments available in this set; they work well for my needs, and for the needs of our family members.
The case securely holds each instrument in its slot; but some instruments are tough to remove from their slots.

More details:

a) Case is soft to the touch and comfortable to hold. There is a tab that I press in to open the case; to close the case, I press both parts of the case together.

b) One compartment (the side with the tab) holds:
--- Nail File
Works well to smooth out my nails after I trimmed them.
--- Fingernail Clipper
The curved blades cut my nails well. The lever has square-shaped divots that give good
grip. It is a bit hard to rotate the lever around the pin; but, with continued use, it should
be easier to open and close the lever. There is no nail file attached to the clipper
--- Nose trimmer
I did not find this to be as good at cutting my nose hairs.
---.Cuticle nipper
This trims my cuticles well.
--- Eyebrow trimmer
This does a decent job in trimming eyebrow
--- Cuspidal/Obtuse push-type broach
I didn't have a need to use this; but several family members used this, and they say it
does an adequate job with their nail polish routine
--- Ear pick
I tried it, but I didn't like the sensation of it scraping against the inside of my ear.

c) The other compartment holds:
--- Toenail clipper
Curved blades cut my toenails well; however, it does not cut my big toenail because
that nail has too much fungus and is too thick. I used a different toenail clipper
recommended by my foot doctor.
--- Oblique nail clipper
I don't use this often for cutting my nail; but I like how easily it cuts the nails. Other
family members love it, and are more familiar in using it
--- Nail cleaning knife
I don't use this; others in my family say it works well to remove the debris under their nails.
--- Tweezers
My family members do not like this, because it doesn't open enough for them to use; so they went
back to using their preferred tweezers
--- Zig Zag callus remover
--- Oblique callus remover
--- Flat callus remover
note: so far, those of us with calluses (including me) do not have them so thick that
they need removal using these instruments.
--- Dead skin fork
a family member used it; she says it is quite useful in removing the loose skin right
around her cuticle area
--- Ear pick (has something like a serrated shape, perhaps)
I much prefer this ear pick; it felt more natural and is like what I used in the past.
--- Acne tool (one end with a circular loop; other end with a pick)
--- Acne tool (one end with a circular loop; other end with a scoop)
None of our family members currently have acne, so we haven't had a chance to try it;
but, we have similar instruments just like these, and those worked well when we've
needed to deal with our skin issues.

Lastly: I would have liked the case to be slightly larger, so that it is easier to remove all these instruments. When I removed all the instruments, some of which I used, others which my family members used, and then put the instruments back into the case, we couldn't get it to close. Finally, we removed the toenail clippers, and that easily closed the case.

Overall, this is a good manicure set, with instruments that I and/or my family members find to be very useful for our particular needs.

text: I've used two other Norelco's. An older 7885 with just straight slots in the heads (but lift and cut). and one with the combination of shaped slots in the head. This new one works better than both. It's quiet. It holds a charge for a long time (I will get over 30 days on a charge). It cuts long whiskers as well as any of the earlier ones, and cuts closer than the earlier ones. I can get a good shave in a little less time than with the 7885 and a lot less time than with my last Norelco with the combination slots (sorry I threw that one away as soon as I got this new one and don't remember the model number). Once you learn how much pressure to use, there is little irritation, the same or less than with the earlier models. I found the spring adjustment in the head to be fine at the default setting, so the "more sensitive" settings won't be useful to me. It seems well made. It's a little flashy, especially the orange accents that I don't prefer. The one gripe i have is it has a converter in the cord and the cord is straight (both of my earlier ones had coiled cords which I prefer). My 7885 has the charger built into the razer so it just had a cord. But given it holds a charge for so long, I can accept that. I will not use the charging stand - it just takes up space. The razer is made in Europe, so hopefully the quality is good. I would recommend this product to anyone that is looking for a good electric razer.

text:
I am using a micro needle roller at .25mm on my scalp for hair growth, or at least hopeful hair growth.

This roller has fewer needles than my other, so I decided to increase the number of stroke repetitions to account for the reduced number of needles, no big deal.

First thing I noticed. The packaging is nice and sterile.

Next, the little cup for the alcohol when sterilizing is really a nice touch.

The quality of the handle is better than anything else I've tried. I think it's some sort of metal. It feels really good in my hand not having plastic.

When using it, it rolls controllably. Very nice feeling of control.

The micro needles seem to be the same length as my other .25mm, so that's accurate.

Overall, this is a high quality product. I like it better than my higher needle count head because the handle is nicer.

I'll see if I actually grow any hair, but the product seems really good, I'd recommend it.

I hope you found this review helpful.

text: I was immediately impressed with the packaging and presentation of this magnetic lash kit.  It was packaged nicely.  Comes with five pairs of 10 magnet lashes, an applicator, and two tubes of magnetic liner.  The lashes are nice, but a little too dramatic for my liking as they are.  I plan on trimming some and applying to the outer half of my eye to see if I can achieve a less dramatic, more comfortable look.  The applicator is a really good quality.  I don't like the liner at all.  It does have a scent and that seems to be common with these magnetic lash kits.  I was able to smell the liner for several hours after application and I can never figure out why the liners have a fragrance at all.  My eyes are extremely sensitive, so this adds to the irritation.  Besides the scent, I did not like the applicator of the liner.  The tip is on the thicker side and the liner is kind of thick as well.  I wasn't able to get a totally smooth line.  The lashes gripped fine to the liner however.  If you have to remove the lashes to reposition, you will likely need another coat of liner to maintain the hold.  If the liner was unscented and had a better applicator, I would have rated higher.  As is, it's a no from me based on the liner alone.

text:
I was interested in trying this night cream because it claims to be especially formulated for oily to normal skin.

PACKAGING:🌟🌟🌟☆☆

The night cream comes in a squeezable tube. I am not a big fan of this type of packaging because as you continue to use it, it becomes harder to get the product out and it can be wasteful. However, this type of packaging is great for traveling. Also, while I am not super impressed with the packaging, it does a nice job at keeping the product fresh.

FIRST IMPRESSION: 🌟🌟🌟🌟☆

It has a light to medium consistency. It has a yellowish color but it does not stain your skin. It has a light scent and it is creamy. It is easy to distribute and apply. It does not irritate or cause redness. While you apply it it has a almost glossy finish and the skin feels soft but as it dries the skin looks matte and it feels smooth. There is no visible residue.

PERFORMANCE:🌟🌟🌟☆☆

After I used it the first time I thought, "Actually this is nice, I think I will really like it", but after using it a few more times, I have changed my opinion. It has great benefits and it excels in some areas but in others I am not very impress with it.

I am impress with the quality and its natural ingredients. It feels really nice when you put it on and the scent is not overbearing which is great. It has no parabens or dangerous chemicals. It is cruelty-free. It does not irritate my skin and it does not cause breakouts. It dries very fast which is great for those long days when I can wait to long to go to bed. It does not have a sticky or greasy finish.

I have been using two different serums and they do not seen to get alone with this cream. So I change it to another and the same problem occur. After my serum is absorb into my skin, I apply this cream but it does not go on smoothly. It looks like brush strokes and it is very patchy. At first, I thought that I was applying to much but the results were the same with less product. This is not a big deal since it will dry either way. But it bother me for some reason.

I enjoy how soft and supple my skin feels as soon as I apply it but then it losses that quality and becomes very matte and it is not longer super soft. My face feels a bit stiff. I would not mind much since it might be helping my skin stay firm. Buw when I wake up in the morning my skin does not feel very moisturize which I do not like. Every time I use it, I want to add a sleeping pack on top for moisture.

However, I have to mentioned that while my skin does not feel supple and hydrated in the morning, it is not greasy or oily either. I am not saying the it will remove all of your sebum/oil but it just makes your skin look matte. I give it points for that but honestly, I rather give my skin hydration at night and I do not care much about looking oily.

OVERALL ASSESSMENT:
Price Point: 🌟🌟🌟🌟☆
Quality/Durability: 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟
Need: 🌟🌟🌟☆☆
Recommendation: 🌟🌟🌟🌟☆

****FTC - I received a discount for this product in exchanged for my honest and unbiased review. Compensation of any kind is never accepted in exchange for a positive review. All opinions expressed are my own based on my experience with the product and not edited by any companies mentioned. This is just my opinion, your experience with this product might be different.I will recommend only the products that I or my family will use. I am disclosing this in accordance with the Federal Trade Commission guidelines concerning the use of endorsements and testimonials in advertising.

If my review was helpful to you, please let me know your thoughts.

text: I've been using this deodorant exclusively since I received it last week. So far I have found the unscented works best. I have sensitive skin, but have had no negative reaction to the scented ones. I've had my husband smell me at the end of the day to see if I stink, and while there has been slight B.O. from one armpit or the other when using the scented ones, the unscented one, when making sure to have full coverage of pit area worked great. Be careful when putting on dark clothes as I've noticed it leaves deodorant type white marks. Currently my armpits are hairy and like I said, works well. All in all I would say 5 stars. I love that they have this trial pack, and it works better than any natural deodorant I have tried! The scented ones don't smell bad, but are kinda hippiesque so I think mixed with perspiration they tend to smell more like an oil trying to cover up a BO scent. Totes worth a try to see how your body respond to it chemically-we are all so different, it takes a while to find one that works for you. UPDATE: I ended up ordering the full size version of the unscented. Still working great after a few weeks. No negative reactions, no stink. I sometimes have to rub it in with my hand a bit as it gets stuck a bit in the hair, but no big deal really. LOVE THIS STUFF.

I'm not running it on batteries ... this is with the unit plugged into my wall outlet. And pay attention to the dimensions - at 16" tall, this is one big mirror! On the plus side, I didn't notice any distortion at all in the magnification and the option for the different light tones is nice.

text: I've tried many sheet masks, but this one is a standout. I was looking for pearl as an ingredient in a sheet mask, and it's hard to find.  I read a lot of reviews before purchasing. Your results may vary, but it wouldn't hurt to try these masks if you've developed broken capillaries.  Certainly there is "plumping", and my skin is bouncy and supple the next morning. I will say that after removing the mask and patting, the essence stays a bit tacky, but with these results I don't mind.  Essence that remains in the packet I use the next night as one of my serum steps.  I certainly will reorder.

text:
I bought this shaving system in the hopes that it could make shaving a bit less painful, as I've always had fairly sensitive skin. In the few months I've owned it, I've been very pleased with the performance and the overall results. Here are a few things I've found...

Pros:
- The moisturizing system does work very well. I get substantially less skin irritation than I have in previous razors that I've used.
- I've discovered I enjoy shaving in the shower. I always tried to avoid mixing shaving before a shower due to the irritation, but this works well.
- The charging and refill system is nice (more on this later).
- Comfortable to hold and use.
- I shave more often using this than any other razor I've used previously.

Cons:
- You'll be compelled to shave daily, since it works best and most efficiently that way. That can be a pro or a con, depending on your lifestyle.
- While the charge and refill station is great, it seems that I always run out of moisturizer before running out of battery, so I have to remember to refill it myself, since they suggest not leaving the razor on charge all the time.
- I haven't found a great shower mirror yet, so I wind up starting my shave prior to the shower, to trim around sideburns and the like.
- Works better as a wet razor than as a dry one.
- While I like the scent of the moisturizer, it took me a bit to get used to it.

Overall, I'm very pleased with this razor and would suggest it to anyone looking for a new electric. If you are OK with the pros and cons, then I say go for it, you won't regret it!

text:
I'm a bigger girl, size 42DD for reference. I've started walking at least 2.5 miles a day wearing a weighted vest. Even in cool weather, with all the hills in my neighborhood, I end up drenched in sweat.

And that means, of course, boob sweat. I have ruined multiple bras and the smell is so gross. Since adding the vest, it's gotten so much worse. It's absolutely disgusting until I get a chance to shower. But I'm a busy mom of two special needs kids, so there are days I get back and can't shower right away, so then I'm feeling gross until I can.

I finally had enough and started looking for a solution. I found this stuff and figured I'd give it a shot. I was realistic in my expectations that it would help a bit, maybe even just help the smell, since is a strenuous activity and not a stroll through a grocery store. I wasn't expecting it to keep me from sweating at all or anything.

I tried it out for the first time today and it blew my expectations out of the water! I was slightly damp, but not even enough to drip. No smell. My bra was almost completely dry! And it's lasted ALL DAY.

I'm one of those ladies who hate wearing a bra at home, but hate how the girls rub without fabric there. I usually tuck whatever shirt I'm wearing under there. But that means that if I end up sweating for some reason, my shirt ends up wet and gross. Today, I realized that after taking off my bra, I wasn't bothered by the skin on skin. The lotion leaves a silky softness on the skin and it kept me comfortable all day.

Now on to the description. The bottle is smaller than I expected, but I learned that a little goes a long way. The lotion goes on creamy and dries quickly. It left no residue on my clothing or any I could see on my skin, except for on my hand from where I applied it. It rinsed off easily. The smell is supposed to be lily, according to the bottle, and I guess it kinda does smell like lily, though the product description listed apple in one place, so I dunno. But it's not a strong scent and it completely disappeared after application.

I love this product. I'm going to be a habitual user from now on and I'm recommending it to all my lady friends!

i finally had enough and started looking for a solution. i found this stuff and figured i'd give it a shot. i was realistic in my expectations that it would help a bit, maybe even just help the smell, since is a strenuous activity and not a stroll through a grocery store. i wasn't expecting it to keep me from sweating at all or anything.

i tried it out for the first time today and it blew my expectations out of the water! i was slightly damp, but not even enough to drip. no smell. my bra was almost completely dry! and it's lasted all day.

i'm one of those ladies who hate wearing a bra at home, but hate how the girls rub without fabric there. i usually tuck whatever shirt i'm wearing under there. but that means that if i end up sweating for some reason, my shirt ends up wet and gross. today, i realized that after taking off my bra, i wasn't bothered by the skin on skin. the lotion leaves a silky softness on the skin and it kept me comfortable all day.

now on to the description. the bottle is smaller than i expected, but i learned that a little goes a long way. the lotion goes on creamy and dries quickly. it left no residue on my clothing or any i could see on my skin, except for on my hand from where i applied it. it rinsed off easily. the smell is supposed to be lily, according to the bottle, and i guess it kinda does smell like lily, though the product description listed apple in one place, so i dunno. but it's not a strong scent and it completely disappeared after application.

i love this product. i'm going to be a habitual user from now on and i'm recommending it to all my lady friends!

text: I live in the South, so it can get pretty hot down here. I also have some skin problems (mild to moderate acne, redness, dry flakes), so I like to wear base makeup whenever I go out. I bought this because I was hoping that combined with a primer and fixing spray, the skin prep pro would help my makeup stay put and not get swept away by the flood of sweat that comes crashing in every summer. The primer and fixing spray I use work well for me (or at least are the best I've tried so far) and sweat is still an issue for me. When this came in, I had high hopes, but unfortunately this is not a miracle product. It did make my face feel tight, so I applied a small amount of moisturizer over it after letting it set but before applying my makeup. I'm a college student, so I walk to class all the time, and after a few classes I could tell that my face was fading and melting as usual. It wasn't a pretty sight. So the next day, to see if it was due to the moisturizer, I just applied the skin prep pro before my makeup. Sadly, I got the same results. I break it out every now and again to see if it's a certain product that it won't work with, but after going through all of my routine taking one product out at a time, I've determined that this just doesn't work for me. I gave this two stars because while it didn't do what I wanted it to, it also didn't make any of my skin problems worse. I also want to give it the benefit of the doubt in case it is helping, just not significantly enough.

text: I think this may have synthetic hair mixed in.  I used my flat iron on the hair at 380 degrees as I normally would on my hair and or extension hair and it seems to have burned some strands making the texture feel very wirey and rough.  I've worn extensions for years and years so I know what to expect and how to treat the hair. This was only after about 2 days of having it in.  I see now it says don't use heat over 180 and don't blow dry it which leads me to believe this cant be 100 Remy human hair.  Human hair extensions can be treated gently like your normal hair with out causing this kind of damage. I went through and trimmed it and deep conditioned which helped a little but overall it feels stiff and dry especially on the ends and upwards 4 or 5 inches.  Its kind of suspicious that 20 inch was under $60.  I'm super sad because its a perfect color match.

text:
I thought I would give this heatless hair curler the ultimate challenge - my daughters thick, 3 ft long hair. My girl plans to grow her hair as long as possible. So, it's important to take special care of it, especially because she only allows it to be cut every few years. As much as possible, I discourage her from using any heated stylers. On special occasions, she wants to have it curled, and I've been looking for something that can handle her volume of hair effectively. I saw this product and was doubtful, but hopeful.

We tried it out a day after she shampooed to allow her hair to fully dry. I watched a YouTube video on how to braid her hair onto the ribbon. It wasn't too difficult to do, but I think I'd have a way harder time trying to do my own hair. Surprisingly, I was able to fit all her hair onto the ribbon and tie it on top her head. She claimed it was fairly comfortable and was able to sleep with it on.

The result in picture 3 was better than expected. The unevenness of the curls are due to my inexperience, but in general, I thought her hair came out very pretty. It lasted for a few hours, but I didn't put any product in, so it could’ve lasted a little longer. Highly recommend!

text:
As a die hard Final Fantasy fan and collector, I was excited to get this set. I believe this is/was for retailers. The box looks like it can be manipulated to create an open display where the blind boxes can be purchased separately. I think this is super cool. The box is black, labeled Dissidia Trading Arts vol. 2 set and has pictures of all five figures.

Inside the outer box are six blind boxes (even though there are only five figures). For those of you who may not know what a blind box is, it's a box where you can't tell what's inside. The UPC and artwork are the same on each box. That's why you can't be assured of getting all five figures. If you get duplicates, you're encouraged to trade them with friends (hence the Trading Arts). I opened five boxes and got all five characters, so I'm going to leave the sixth box unopened. I'll display it with the figures because the artwork is cool. I'll try to upload pictures of the boxes.

There is some assembly required, but not too much. Mostly connecting the upper torso to the legs and connecting weapons. I had a tough time assembling them because their pieces fit so tightly together. I had to get my husband to help with two of them. Finding a safe area to grip was part of it. You'll want to be careful around Firion's ponytail and the hilts of their swords. They can be snapped off with little effort.

I agree with the other reviewer that Terra/Tina is difficult to put and keep on her stand, but I've got a solution. Any doll stand that fits around her waist will solve that problem. Tina is about 5" tall, so I'm going to get a mini doll stand where the waist gripper is 2.5" high. (I had to do the same thing for the Warrior of Light.)

It takes a while to get them put together. Seriously, Firion has like five weapons you need to attach and Cecil has one that attaches to his lower back. You have to keep it from falling off as you squeeze it under his cape, but once assembled, they are something to look at. These are actual Square Enix figures (not knock offs) and they look it. The detail in their hair and clothing is amazing! My favorites are Cecil and of course, Sephiroth. Be mindful of his Masamune katana, it's accidentally knocked a couple of my other figures over.

Overall, this set is an awesome deal. If you bought all the figures separately, not only would the boxes be opened (necessary to find out who was inside) you'd spend over $200. Sephiroth is around $60 by himself. Terra/Tina is more than that and not only would you not have Bartz/Butz (I couldn't find him separately), you wouldn't get the sixth blind box either. I highly recommend this set to any Final Fantasy fan.

text:
As someone who's never grown a beard or mustache (I can go maybe a day or two but after that it drives me crazy), this has to be the most comfortable electric shaver I've ever used. The three pivoting heads move over facial contours very easily, and I appreciate that it's quieter than others I've used. In spite of that, it took a week or so to feel like it was doing a good job - perhaps because my face needed time to adjust (as claimed in the manual) or because it takes me a while to figure out how to use it most effectively - but I'm happy with the job it does. The neck has been the hardest area, and I've still got a few rough spots on the jawline, but it's much less than at first. The only real "negative" is the very small trimmer, which is placed rather awkwardly and doesn't seem to work very well.

Another nice plus is the LED display showing exactly how much time is left before needing to be recharged, and each charge seems to last much longer than others I've used (betweeen 2 to 3 weeks, and it only took an hour to fully charge). You can't use it plugged in, but it will allow you to plug it in for a "quick charge." The display also shows when to clean it (about every other use) and the heads flip up for easy cleaning under running water (much easier than using the tiny brush). It looks great, too - very sleek and cool and stylish - with a nice little charging stand, which looks good enough to leave out on the counter (minus the cord). It also comes with a "charging pod" which will be good for travelling.

Overall, I'm very pleased with it, although $240 is pretty steep. It's still not "as close as a blade" but I've given up believing that any electric shaver truly can be, but it's a whole lot more convenient that using shaving cream. (Others have complained about the "excessive" and difficult packaging, and I must agree completely on that point - use a knife carefully or scissors).

text:
This is a “dressed up” version of the Wahl “LifeProof” shaver (which I also reviewed). It has a more upscale look and feel to it, plus adds a battery gauge to show the charge level. However, the shave quality is the same – OK, but not a standout.

The case feels more solid than the LifeProof and, while it’s more of a traditional look, I think they did a nice job with the overall aesthetics. It is pretty grippy even with wet hands. It does not, however, feel as comfortable to hold as the more sculpted models such as most of the Panasonic shavers and some Braun shavers.

The shave quality of this model is identical to that of the LifeProof: very Remington in how it feels (close, but rather aggressive). Performance is best on the easy spots (cheek) but not as good on the more trickier spots (neck and jawline). The flex head helps with dealing with face contours but it lacks enough travel to do an excellent quality job.

The aggressiveness of the shave is dialed down when using it wet. It needs a very, very thin layer of shave cream (the gel style creams work well) or the head gets bogged down.

Love the trimmer! The positioning could be improved to make it easier to use, but it cuts very cleanly on sideburns and ‘shash trimming.

The controls are clean, easy to read and tell you all you need to know (charge and battery status).

I like the feel of this better than the Wahl LifeProof, but the shave quality keeps this at the same rating (average, which is 3 stars). It’s a very workable shaver, but for the price there are better choices available from Panasonic (anything in their Arc line) or, for less money, from Remington.

I really was looking for something that was soft but cleaning. I looked into the soft 310,000 bristle brushes, but after reading reviews it would take way too long to dry to be able to use everyday. The Elishacoy brush is super soft but small enough to dry during the night and during the day. I use it in the morning and the night with my cleanser. I only use it for about 10 seconds on each area of my face. Avoid the eyes the skin is very fragile.

PROS:
Soft brush
Two different settings for speed
Fits in hand well
Silicone cover battery to make it waterproof
Ultra-affordable for quality
Made pores appear smaller

CONS:
Korean brand you can't go to store to buy brush replacements

•i tried to put clear polish over the nail to preserve the manicure look but it still looks really bad.
•I haven’t been able to put on any nails without having severe air bubbles.
•there’s hardly any variation of size so hope you have small hands when you order these.
•the pink is a little dark and looks unnatural especially with so many air bubbles.
•this is a personal preference but I don’t like the sharpness of the shape, my boyfriend complains that I scratch him and I scratch myself too unless I file the sides to become round.
•they break easily!

I would give it a 4-star rating. One star took off because of the lack of instructions in English.

text:
I'm a person who has used several types of clippers in the past, both corded and cordless and I've gotta say this is one of the best! Originally discovered it on Amazon when I was in the market to replace my former cordless clipper and was impressed by the high rating. Now I'm singing its praises too.

I've been using it for over a year now and would definitely buy another if I had to... but I don't! It's still going strong with a powerful battery and the suction of loose hair even allows me to shave in the car when I'm pressed for time. I would say as long as you shave in the proper direction (where the blade cuts first and the suction thingy follows) the shaver will suck in at least 85% of the loose hair it cuts. Usually I have very little hair left on the sink after I shave. The fact that it's cordless is a plus and the device gives just enough options with the adjustable guard and the precision blade. A full charge last me long enough for me to forget when was the last time I charged it and I love the fact that it's a good size and weight for travel. A great buy at a good price!

text:
Having used the disposable razors now for 25 plus years i decided to try an electric razor again, given the fact your considered a terrorist if you have shaving cream cans in your luggage while traveling, the electric razor seemed like a viable solution. After much reviewing i decided to go with the 9160xl at christmas, it was almost march before i recharged it, and after a few days of use, i was used to how it works, its much faster to complete a shave, trim my mustache and gote, and off i go with no worries of water on my shirt, or shaving cream to wipe off. You can also shave while walking around as your not tied to the sink and running water to clear off the razor. The key to any battery operated device is to properly charge it when you first place it on the bathroom sink. And do not return it to the charger until the shaver shows low battery, that way you keep the battery life good. And yes its quiet. Does not sound like a small airplane nor does it make a high pitched whinning noise. Comments about not having power or cutting of skin are ridiculous, i have never had a problem, all i do is clean it out each use, and rinse the head out when the razor indicates it wants a water cleaning. Simple as that, follow the instructions, and youll be fine. Remember the key to the battery as that is the life of the razor, same rules apply to your cell phone.
Good luck.

text:
My friend introduced me to epilating about 5 years ago. She had a double headed one from Spain it was amazing you could barely feel a thing. I searched for one similar everywhere and did some research that said the 40 dollar epilators do just as good as the more expensive ones. So I settled and bought a 40 dollar one, it did the job yes and I got use to how it worked (I usually waxed though since the epilator never removed every thing) Well it died about 4 months ago after, I decided that I wanted an epilator that would last and actually get the job done.

I went back to researching, I read so many reviews and many of you probably have and will. It is from my experience it depends on the person and the day that makes the review (I am having normal day.)

I decided in the to buy the Braun 7281 and crossed my fingers. Since I had grown use to my last epilator I was extremely surprised that this one felt so much better and barely noticed any pain; as well as I don't have to wax anymore saving so much more money now by spending more on the better epilator. But I decided I would give myself about 6 weeks and see how it did pain wise. It didn't feel the worst but it did sting for a few minutes and then I got use to it again. I must say that it still didn't hurt as bad as the 40 dollar one did and it still did just as good as a wax would. (Any epilator is going to sting if you haven't waxed or epilated in a 6 weeks +, I recommend waxing if you have sensitive skin before you jump back into epilating.) In the end I would even say this one is equal if not better then one my friend had and would recommend it to anybody. Added bonus is that it comes with many different tops as well as an electric razor.

If you are still on the fence about buying this one verses the 40 dollar one you have also been looking at. Buy the 40 dollar one first and in a few months when it dies, come back and buy this one. But if you want to save yourself time and money then make the jump and buy the Braun 7281 you won't regret it.

Also if you aren't familiar with how to use and epilator read the instructions it doesn't work like a razor and if you use it like a razor it will hurt. I feel it is the only reason people would complain about this epilator.

text:
I have been around this product most of my adult life and have used it in every conceivable way for skin problems like you will read
in all of these reviews posted. However I have had a nagging problem over the years that keep reoccurring and I had been to specialist
to have it taken care of with little results. The problem I am referring to is a nagging ear infection that I got swimming in the Sea of Cortez
in Mexico years ago. Then one night I thought to try utter balm on it so I dabbed some on a q tip and swabbed it inside of my ear canal
(be careful don't go to far in) then the next morning I could feel some of the balm in there but when it finished absorbing the ear infection
was completely healed and has not come back!!! I will certainly be taking this with me when I return to Mexico.

text: I thought i had rosacea on my nose, it would always get red and puffy all the time, I went to the dermatologist and he confirmed that it indeed was. I figured my dad and his brothers had it too. I thought it was pimples or zits so I would try to pop them only to find out I couldn't, leaving this really bad dark scars behind. I couldn't deal with it anymore it got so bad to the point I could not go out in public with out a band aid covering the big red shiny bumps it would create on my nose. I tried changing my diet and it worked but I could not go on longer than a week depriving myself from my favorite foods. As soon as I this certain food it came back. I looked up reviews online trying to find creams that would help reduce my rosacea and the dark scars it left me. This cream would pop up everywhere in every website I would go on. I saw it was also really cheap compared to this other creams that aren't any cheaper than $80. I bought this cream 2 weeks ago and been using it ever since. My nose has not turned red at all since, I have not gotten any of the big disgusting bumps I used to get and it has reduced my dark scars slowly. It has also helped my skin looked younger since it used to look really dry and wrinkly from upclose due to all the previous acidic creams and lotions my doctors would prescribe me. I recommend this cream to any person who has rosacea and has a bit of money to spare, I believe it is worth a try since I took a gamble buying it and it has paid off. I will update this review if anything changes.

text:
I have fine, wavy shoulder-length hair. Likes to get especially tangly around the back. Anyone else in this camp knows that finding products/brushes/styles for this type can be borderline excruciating, but this brush works very well!

Pros:
The "naturally conditions oil" claim seems to be a valid one, i do feel that i need to condition less frequently and oils are more evenly distributed from root to end using this product
Experiencing significantly less tearing, snarls and general despair than when brushing with other types
My hair is shiny and smooth, and I have to spend less time styling if i use this!

Cons:
Has less "grab" than non-bristle brushes; I often have to comb first or separate sections more than i would have to with a standard brush with longer "teeth." That being said, the end result is worth it!

text:
I bought this for a new (refurb) 80D that I just got a little while ago. That 80D was factory refurbed and it, and the sensor, was very clean when I got. I put this case on (a black one) the camera almost the same day that I got the camera, and I actually didn't have much time to use it over the next couple of weeks, but then, one day I was out trying to do some shooting of planes and BIF. When I looked at the images, I noticed a large piece of dust on the image (which turned out to be on the sensor). Looking back at some test images I made when I first got the camera, they were clean. Anyway, so I used a blower and was able to get rid of the large dust, but then I did a white wall shot to check the sensor and I was SHOCKED that there was a lot of dust on the sensor. What was weird is most of the dust looked like it was around the perimeter of the sensor. I used a sensor dust brush and that got rid of some of the dust, but I eventually had to send the camera to the Canon service center to get it cleaned off.

To be clear/honest, I am not 100% sure about this, but I was trying to figure out how that happened and I am guessing/theorizing that maybe the case, which is rubber, might have been attracting dust. The case is very close around the base of the lens mount, so I am thinking that, maybe, there was dust attracted around the lens mount, and when I unmounted the lens (which I did once or twice and always with the camera pointed downward), some dust would get onto the sensor.

Mind you, I think this case does provide some protection for the body, so that part is ok.

text:
I really don't understand people who think the flavor is so heinous. It's a little strange, but nothing that's going to make you gag. I also wouldn't say that it tastes are smells like synthetic chemicals. These come pretty fresh sealed inside the packages and are at first really easy to peel and loosen the fibers. Once it dries out, you will need to employ the water-soaking method, which makes it simple enough. Just make sure it soaks for at least an hour for very easy handling. I've been using the same stick for months. It usually takes about a week or two for the end to get ratty enough to cut and start over again. I wouldn't say this stick eliminates flossing. It depends on the way your teeth are situated. I have some problem spots that I absolutely need to use floss for. That being said, I've noticed a significant reduction of plaque that is removed with a floss after brushing with a miswak (vs. after brushing with a good conventional toothbrush or even a sonicare toothbrush). In my personal experience, a miswak works better than a Sonicare. However, if you like the convenience of the Sonicare, stick to that. It depends on each person's habits and personality. You can damage your enamel with a Sonicare, so please be careful and follow the instructions carefully. You really don't have a problem with hurting gums or the enamel with a miswak. It is also the perfect travel toothbrush.

Pros: Lasts a long time, is completely natural, no need for toothpaste, minimal flossing, travels easily, gentle on teeth and gums while getting into nooks and crannies better than a traditional western brush, dental study shows improvement of gingivitis with regular use of miswak.

Cons: Awkward handling for back of teeth, must peel to get a new layer, some people don't like the taste, not easy for children to use.

text: OK - so I'm a fingerstyle guitar player (James Taylor / Chet Atkins wannabe). Fingernail care is an annoying part of the business, I'm afraid. And unfortunately, I have a nail that splits often. Silk reinforcements with resin and activator has been a lifesaver for maintaining nails, and this sanding block is part of the process. I use a diamond file to keep the fingernail ends shaped and the sanding block makes the finished ends smooth and snag free. Snagging is NOT good for finger picking guitar! I'm still learning to use it, but it also does a great job of smoothing the nail before applying silk and resin on the split nail and Sally Hanson's triple hard titanium polish on the rest of my nails. Yeah, I get the other guys ribbing me over my shiny nails - which is pretty obvious when they see both hands together - only the right hand gets the fussing. But then they hear me play and the ribbing stops. My only complaint about this would be useful life - it seems to wear down rather quickly. But I'm probably not being fair - that diamond nail file lasts forever - can't expect a soft foam sanding block to last that long.

text:
I'll be honest - for the price, I wasn't expecting much. I bought this thinking that if the tool for cleaning under the toenails was usable and didn't snap off the first time I used it, I'd consider it a win.
I can honestly say I'm blown away by the quality of these items. They're heavy and solid, and appear to have no plastic whatsoever (other than the grips on the clippers). They actually feel like quality dental or medical tools in terms of weight, finish, and balance. The nippers are strong, sharp, precise, and perfectly aligned. They require a bit of getting used to for using, but they snipped even my thick old gnarly big toenail with almost no effort. They worked pretty well on dead skin/calluses too, but to be honest they're so sharp I'm afraid to try much with that - I'll just stick to the cuticle removing tool, which is also sharp if somewhat less easy to use on my own toes (works great on my fingers though).

I am extremely impressed - these would be a value at even twice or three times the price.

text:
This eyelash curler is well made, and it's a good one. With that being said I will have to say that after I read all the reviews on it before I bought it, I was expecting something fabulous, and really, this is just a really good eyelash curler, nothing more. I have owned eyelash curlers for many years and my last one was a Revlon brand that I paid less than $5.00 for and for the money, it was almost as good as the Bella one. I have no regrets on buying the Bella curler, and if you're considering it, I would say go for it, it does what it's supposed to, but it won't be a glorious experience by any means as it may appear from some of the other reviews. It will pinch your eyelid if you don't grasp your lashes correctly. It will crimp your lashes if you don't grasp/squeeze properly, as any eyelash curler will do, and as some of the other reviewers stated, the curl isn't long-lasting. As I said this is a good item, just don't expect miracles.

I'm hoping the pads will be more durable than the other brands I have owned and only time will tell on that.

The rest of this feedback I'm aiming at the guys, since it's hard to believe any woman would let her feet get as disgustingly calloused as we men would. Guys, the question I bet is at the top of your list is "How powerful is it?" I'm not gonna lie - to really get rid of thick callouses is going to require patience. Powered by four AA batteries, the spinning head-wheel goes at a good speed, but if you give the unit anything more than moderate pressure against your foot, the wheel will slow or stop. That means you'll need to do longer sessions with lighter pressure. But it DOES WORK! Just don't expect to get to your goal of socially-acceptable feet after five minutes of usage. I've done three 15 minute sessions just on one foot and I'm very nearly there.

Other than that, I'm happy with this unit.

text: I purchased two of these together on a deep discount (half price or something). Kept one at home and one in my car for backup, shaving during commuting or at work. I mixed use between the two and a bic safety razor pretty regularly. The two electric shavers both stopped working within a few days of each other during month 10. They appeared to be fully charged but the motor became very slow and then just didn't start up again. A few times I heard a quick buzz sounding like the motor was trying but couldn't start. I think these are probably brushed motors and the brushes just failed. I took one apart and the battery was fine (just a normal Lithium cell). In the end, it's not that bad of a value - $10 a pop and lasts almost a year. Costs less than the replacement blade head on most name brand electric shavers and less than 3 or 4 disposable safety razors blade sets from the name brands. But I hate filling the landfill, so that's a bummer.

text:
This color is pretty good for the price. There are a few pros and cons to it that I will explain below, but overall I don't think I will buy it again.

Pro:
Comes with 2 containers which is enough color for any length hair
The ability to adjust the shade you want by adding conditioner to the color
A little bit goes a VERY long way
Price is very reasonable
When it fades, it fades all the way out so it doesn't look like some weird shade of orange when it starts to wash out
You don't need any heat to get the color to stick
It doesn't smell like anything

Cons:
Fades INCREDIBLY fast - I had it in for maybe a week or two before it washed all the way out
You have to use a ton of conditioner to create a light soft pink color - the size of a dime with about 2 cups of conditioner do the trick
It will dye everything it comes in contact with - use thick gloves
If you are diluting the color with conditioner, it will take some time to sit on your hair
If you are diluting the color, you'll never need both bottles - it would take 5 years to use all of it

I think overtone color conditioner is an easier system to use and the color stays longer.

text:
I love these! They puff up in liquid like little marshmallows, and are easy to peel apart to form the full mask. It works for my face size/shape just fine (a smidge small for men maybe). They appear to be high quality, and I love that they're individually wrapped! I throw these in my travel bags to use with my serums and lotions, and give them away for people interested. I've used these with blended aloe vera, gels, serums, hyaluronic acid, high moisture "lotions" and so much more.

I usually remove the packaging, drop the "tablet" into the well-mixed up liquid/gel concoction I chose that day, and wait for it to puff up. Then I peel it apart to form the full mask, plop it back in the liquid/gel to absorb some more, and throw it in the fridge (because I like the masks cold). I use these after showering and cleaning my face, and watch tv or read with it on. Once I feel the edges start to feel slightly dry, I peel it off and massage the rest of it into my skin, and top off with another moisturizer before bed.

I liked these so much, I bought a package for my mom too! Do note, the ones I received were actually in a blue bag, with blue writing on the individual packaging.

text:
I needed a new gray wig and after browsing through the same popular brands on Amazon I decided to take a chance and give this wig a shot...regardless of having only one (one star) review. But I’m glad I did though! The other reviewer mentioned that this unit was too thin but if you look at the pictures I posted, I can assure you it’s not. And the color was spot on; It was the perfect amount of gray and silver and platinum that changed shades depending on the different lighting. The part was decent but still needed some extra plucking and some concealer to blend the lace. (I didn’t have it glued down for the photos so you can still see the lace). As for the styling, personally I like more of a beach or body wave so I need to redo the style but I did straighten it out a bit to relax the curls and it took the heat very well.
I only had two complaints: the roots laid sooo flat and the hairline was way too round. The hairline was pre plucked but way too evenly thinned so it still looked kinda fake and the roundness had me pluckin hairs for what seemed like hours lol. I guess I could YouTube some tricks on how to give this girl some volume because now that’s the only thing missing from her!
For the price, you get more than what you paid for...just be ready to spend some quality time giving her some TLC :)

text:
I will admit I was extremely skeptical of the claim that these cloths could remove makeup with just water but they do! I would even go as far to say that they do a better job than a cotton cloth with soap on it. I have sensitive skin so using harsh soaps or cleansers is not an option for me. Typically I would struggle to get my mascara completely removed. I would always end up with raccoon eyes requiring me to scrub harder than I would like on the delicate skin below my eyes and I don’t even use waterproof mascara. That problem is a thing of the past now that I have these washcloths. I am able to remove all my makeup with minimal effort and no scrubbing.

The cloths are very soft and feel good against your skin whether they are wet or dry. I used one the other day as a sweat rag while I was working out because they are so absorbent; it worked great. The stitching looks solid and they have a nice thick feel to them. I haven’t been using them long enough to run them through the wash but they feel very well made so I am not worried about them falling apart in the wash. I thought they would collect so much makeup that I would need to wash them after almost every use but that is not the case a quick rinse in clean water and they are good to go.

The color is true to the picture displayed although I would call it closer to sea foam green than blue.

My only complaint is I could do without the rather large tag that stretches across the corner. I will probably look at the stitching a little closer to see if I can safely remove it without ruining them.

I received this product for a discount in exchange for my honest review. However, I would like you to know that I personally value reviews when purchasing products online and this truly is my honest opinion.

text:
I’ve been using versa products for years, as that’s what’s typically used in standalone self tanning units in my area.

Maybe just because I’m so obituuated, I don’t mind the characteristics smell, and this formulation is really thick and rich, but still quite easy to spread. It also definitely changes your color in a very pronounced way. This one is not a gradual or faint gradation. It will absolutely transfer onto your clothes, because it has instant bronzing agents in it. Sometimes that’s pretty useful to see if you’re getting streaks— you can work to address it as you go. But I don’t love the instant bronzers because they always, always transfer to my clothing. This one, like all of them, will do that, particularly if you sweat.

I guess self tanning products have really vaulted up in price, but at the current listed price of 44.95, this does seem expensive, though it is generously sized and works really well. If you like the idea but want to save money, maybe look into the newer tanning drops. By getting the darkest ones, you can just add a couple to your lotion to get an incremental tan because a little goes a long way. But if you like the appearance of a tan while you’re waiting for the pigment to appear over the course of a day or night, this one will give you the longer-term results along with the instant gratification
