I own a foot filer (Amope-style) already and was intrigued by an electric nail filer. The product came well-packaged and with some toe seperators, which was a nice bonus. I also appreciated that it comes in a plastic, TSA-type storage pouch.

As for the product itself, there is still some work to do before it's a great product. Firstly, the fact that they do not offer refills yet is a huge deterrent. I've only used mine a few times and the heads are still functioning adequately, but soon I'm sure they won't (particularly the filer) and then it will be useless until they offer refill file heads.

Secondly, the file head itself could be of better quality, in terms of filing power. The buff and shine ones are pretty good, but not amazing, as it still takes a little longer than I'd expect to get great results.

Finally, because everything just work "okay", the end result is perfectly fine. Fine is not bad, but I can achieve a better, more fine tuned result with a quality multi-way filing block in only slightly more time. Changing the file heads vs using 1 block for my hands, it's a no brainer I'm better off just using the block. I have found the buff & shine heads useful on my toes, though, for a quick shine boost when I'm in-between polish and letting my toenails breathe. For my toes, the speed of the machine is a little helpful to be bent over lslghtly less long.

All in all it is a pretty okay little device, but for the price it's not quite "ready for prime time". I can only see this being beneficial to those with hand problems, such as carpal tunnel, arthritis, etc, because then not having to grip a block and make a repeated motion for so long would be helpful.

I did receive a discount on my purchase, but my review is honest & unbiased.

text: I love Michel Design Works, especially the lotions. I have about a dozen of them, but this fragrance is the first one that’s disappointed me. text: I really enjoy using sheet masks and since adopting a Korean skincare routine, they have become a staple for me. I've tried out a lot of different ones at varying price points and I have to say the quality of this sheet mask is absolutely amazing. I have very oily and sensitive skin and so I'm always wary about trying a new product especially ones that are targeted at moisturizing. While oily skin needs moisture, it is sometimes hard to find the right balance in a product that will moisturize without making me look even more oily. text:
I really wanted to love this. It seemed like the perfect alternative to my aging WaterPik brand water flosser. It's smaller, light weight and doesn't have a huge tank and curly hose like the Water Pick. BUT,
it is a poor substitute. First the water tank is pretty small and I usually run out of water before I'm done.
The tank has a design flaw. There is a curved hose that is inside the tank that delivers water to the head. If you don't hold the flosser in the right position, the hose is out of the water and doesn't work. I can turn the head, but I have to turn off the flosser to do that.
It makes a loud noise, especially when the water gets low, that is irritating to me. The Water Pick makes a noise too, but this is louder and more shrill.
This set included little plastic tips that go on the end of the flosser head, but it constantly falsl off and tries to go down the drain so I just leave it off.
It comes with several replacement heads. That's good. It has two settings. One is pretty hard the other is very gentle. You have to push the on button quickly after it starts to get to the gentle setting which is not as easy as it sounds when you are squirting water in your mouth and trying not to make a mess.
It's not perfect. It may be okay for travel, but I guess I'll keep my WaterPik until it dies completely (it's 10 years old) and then get another WaterPik. In the meantime I'll keep trying to like this one.

text: It is such a simple idea. Take a manual razor and an electric razor and create a hybrid electrical manual razor at a cheap price. Forget about buying any kind of electrical razor or manual razor again. This thing works using a single AAA battery (and is worth investing in some rechargable AAA batteries) which slips into the base by pulling out the end piece. Just press the green button and the razor starts to vibrate. This vibration stimulates the skin and gets those hairs to prick up a little bit so that razor blades (all three baldes, i.e - Mach III/M3) catch better creating a cleaner smoother cut. To be honest the old Mach III model was absolutely great but the M3 Power razor just upgrades that razor into a totally new device. There is simply nothing on the market that remotely resembles this kind of product... nothing. I would not hesitate in sending anyone down to the local drug store with a $20 to pick up this wonderful product that... quite frankly... just making shaving enjoyable again!

text:
This is my first foray into essential oils and I'm liking it a lot. I'm using the oil in two ways: for aroma and, in theory, for its physiological properties. I like diffusers a lot but am too cheap and suspicious of additives to buy them often. I've been using this lavender oil along with some safflower oil and rubbing alcohol in my bedroom and it adds a nice, luxurious touch to bedtime. I've sprinkled a bit on my body pillow, too, when I feel like I want a much stronger dose of the relaxation.

I read somewhere that mixing it with a carrier oil (I'm using rosehip oil) and rubbing it on the bottom of your feet can help with insomnia, so I've been doing that. I don't know if it's working, but it is making my feet feel softer, so that's something. I haven't experienced any respiratory or skin irritation with this product.

The bottle is a dark brown color so that the light can't get to the oil and break it down. They sent a dropper along with the oil, which I appreciated since I didn't have others. Next I'm going to try to make some kind of reusable sachet for my drawers--I just need to figure out what scent to mix it with first.

I was fortunate to receive this product at a free or discounted rate in exchange for my honest review. I am always conscious and aware of the number of items and possessions I bring into my home and try to make mindful decisions, because The Life-Changing Magic of Tidying Up told me to. For this reason, I only accept items that I feel will serve me, my family, and my needs. I do not accept items that seem useless or that do not speak to me. If I post a positive review it is because I had a positive experience. If I have only positive experiences, I chalk that up to a successful exercise in mindful purchasing and decision-making. If I have a negative experience, as with any Amazon product, I reach out to the seller or manufacturer and seek assistance or replacement before deciding on a rating.

text:
UPDATE - 09/30/16 ========================================
Still loving this moisturizer and just ordered another package. This stuff goes on well, absorbs quickly into the skin and doesn't leave a greasy feeling! My skin feels truly moisturized. I love using it on my hands and feet before bedtime, this way it really absorbs into my skin overnight.
Love it, love it, love it!

========================================================
Not many people know this, or give it another thought sometimes - anything you put on your skin gets absorbed through the skin and ends up in the blood stream. This is why I am rather careful when picking my skin care products. I have been staying away from most hand creams for a while now just because for the most part there's at least a few potential carcinogens lurking among the ingredients. Even though the big famous brand creams make your skin feel wonderful the question is if the trade off is worth it.

Unfortunately, even so called organic products aren't always that safe. Provided, manufacturers of this goodness are disclosing all the ingredients on the label (and not hiding anything) this cream looks safe enough to eat. Therefore, I have been happily slathering it all over my hands (and any other body parts needing additional moisture) in the past few weeks and I am quite pleased with the results.

This white fluffy goodness feels surprisingly light and absorbs quickly into the skin. I must admit, it is a tad thinner in texture than I had expected but on the other hand this lightness ensures there's no oily residue left on your hands. The cream has no scent whatsoever which is a definite positive for those who are sensitive or don't particularly appreciate scented products. I'm adding a drop of essential oil of my choice (whatever I feel like that day) when I apply the cream if I feel like smelling something nice. Basically this cream is your blank canvas and leaves much room for creativity.

One thing I would probably change about this product is the packaging. (At least maybe as an option for buyers to choose from) I really think that tubes are better for hand creams - way easier to dispense and so much more hygienic (sticking your fingers into the container doesn't really help keep it germ free). Other than that I am happy to give this product my approval.

I purchased this product at a discount in exchange for an honest and unbiased review, all opinions are based on my own personal experience with the product.

unfortunately, even so called organic products aren't always that safe. provided, manufacturers of this goodness are disclosing all the ingredients on the label (and not hiding anything) this cream looks safe enough to eat. therefore, i have been happily slathering it all over my hands (and any other body parts needing additional moisture) in the past few weeks and i am quite pleased with the results.

this white fluffy goodness feels surprisingly light and absorbs quickly into the skin. i must admit, it is a tad thinner in texture than i had expected but on the other hand this lightness ensures there's no oily residue left on your hands. the cream has no scent whatsoever which is a definite positive for those who are sensitive or don't particularly appreciate scented products. i'm adding a drop of essential oil of my choice (whatever i feel like that day) when i apply the cream if i feel like smelling something nice. basically this cream is your blank canvas and leaves much room for creativity.

one thing i would probably change about this product is the packaging. (at least maybe as an option for buyers to choose from) i really think that tubes are better for hand creams - way easier to dispense and so much more hygienic (sticking your fingers into the container doesn't really help keep it germ free). other than that i am happy to give this product my approval.

i purchased this product at a discount in exchange for an honest and unbiased review, all opinions are based on my own personal experience with the product.

text:
I received 3 packs of "Revive Rinse Free Bathing Wipes". Each pack contains 12 wipes.

I wanted to try these wipes because I thought they could be a better option for me than regular baby wipes for my period accident clean ups & they are pretty nice (not perfect).

I have heavy extended length menstrual periods and have always used regular baby wipes for any messy / accident clean ups. That way I can just throw them away after I'm done with them.

These bathing wipes are way larger than a typical baby wipe. They are white in color and have a light perfume type of scent (which is probably the Chamomile Extract).

They can be microwaved (for no more than 15 seconds according to the pack) if you wanted them warm but I just used them "as is" right out of the packs.

They are wet enough to get the job done but not dripping wet (leaking) everywhere. They are also a nice thickness and soft to the touch.

They didn't irritate my sensitive skin at all. They also aren't soapy at all, they left my skin feeling okay (clean) maybe a little bit sticky but nothing that I couldn't tolerate.

I do like these wipes overall because they are large in size and affordable.

text: This product is surprisingly thin and absorbs quickly into skin. My hands are prone to splitting and dry skin areas thanks to poor sloughing off of dead skin cells courtesy of a genetic skin condition.  I also spend a lot of time with my hands in water or washing my hands all day and my knockles always crack, especially in the winter and/or if I have used many liquid hand soaps. This crack repair "lotion" does make a difference quickly if you use at least  2x a day. If I missed an application, I could tell since the fingers started to split again. The formula stung slightly on some or the deeper cuts on my hands, but it wasn't too bad. I put this product on  first and then put on my regular hand lotion. My only dislike is the smell of this product. It doesn't smell gross, but doesn't smell pleasant either. It absorb so quickly that I sometimes have to apply a double dose lately with dry heat running in the house.  The small size tube is easy to stash in a purse, pocket or desk drawer.

text:
Bought these as a test to see if the magnetic eyelashes are worth it. I’d never tried them before and figured, what the hell. As a long time eyelash fan, I completely understand the idea of spending hours on make up just to have that final step of putting glue-on lashes ruin it all, they’re not easy... so the appeal was here to give this new way a try.

Let me be frank, they’re terrible and not worth the money. I’ll take my chances on the glue...

To explain why... I can’t speak to all brands but the magnetic eyeliner for this has not been perfected yet. Despite shaking the container vigorously for 10-20 shakes per instructions, the eyeliner is thick. Even using my tiny regular eyeliner brush to apply I still ended up with thick raccoon lines. Fine, a night out it might be a little thicker than normal... I looked past it... however when it came to putting the lashes on...

They provided a metal applicator?! Why? The eyelashes stuck to it and make applying it to the eyeliner difficult. I switched to a plastic applicator that I get from my regular glue on lashes. That made it a little easier. However, you have to be excessively careful not to get the eyeliner on your actual eyelashes or it will stick to them.

Okay, second try, reapply trying to make the eyeliner thinner and avoiding my eyelashes, waiting a little while for the eyeliner to fully dry, bam they stick! However, they’re a little higher than my lash line so I go to adjust them, the eyeliner flakes off and there’s a chunk of eyeliner missing (it’s attached to the magnet of course.) I take my small eyeliner brush, fill in the hole, wait for it to dry and place it right back.

let me be frank, they’re terrible and not worth the money. i’ll take my chances on the glue...

okay, second try, reapply trying to make the eyeliner thinner and avoiding my eyelashes, waiting a little while for the eyeliner to fully dry, bam they stick! however, they’re a little higher than my lash line so i go to adjust them, the eyeliner flakes off and there’s a chunk of eyeliner missing (it’s attached to the magnet of course.) i take my small eyeliner brush, fill in the hole, wait for it to dry and place it right back.

text:
I’m a sucker for a good brush set. It’s even better when it comes with case and a sponge!

So the case is very sturdy and feels well made. You can use it as a brush holder and you can use it store brushes. I am using the shorter half as a brush holder and find that it blends in nicely with my other holders.

Now I’ll do a brief breakdown of the brushes and sponge.

The lip brush is great and it blends in my lipsticks really nice.

There are three brushes for eyes- eyeshadow brush, blending brush and angled liner. I have tried these and they are very soft and work very well.

The powder brush is a bit smaller than I would personally like for powder. It’s not quite dense but in a brush that is used for powder products that’s okay. When I’m buffing in the powder I don’t want the brush to be too dense or it will move the foundation around. I’ve been using this brush for blush because of it’s smaller shape and it’s working great.

Now the final two- the foundation brush and the sponge. I decided to do half of my foundation with the brush on one side, and the other half with the sponge on the other side.

The foundation brush is a typical brush of it’s kind. It did not make the foundation look streaky and applied very smooth.

The sponge is the most interesting piece in this set. It doesn’t feel super soft like a Beauty Blender or a Real Techniques sponge. It feels like a softer stress ball and feels a bit rubbery. I’ve never had a sponge like this. I soaked the sponge in water and drained the excess as I would do with any sponge. It felt heavier but did seem to soften a bit and did expand in size. I dabbed the foundation on my face and pressed the sponge around to press the product in and I got a really smooth finish. The sponge did not seem to absorb much product.

Overall I think it’s a very nice set!

I purchased this product at a discount in exchange for an honest review. I did not get paid to do this review and my opinions are 100% honest.

text:
I’m writing this review for people like me who struggle with recurring chalazions and styes:
I started getting chalazion 5 years ago on both eyes and I don’t know what even started it. It was very frustrating because these bumps would form on my eyelid and nothing worked on them. I got surgery once to get rid of one on my lower eye lid. They stayed for months and warm compresses and eye massages that doctors recommended me did nothing.
I read many research papers to see what I can do to help my condition and here is a summary:
1. this eye wash helped so much. Since I started using this eye wash two months ago my chalazions are almost gone and very unnoticeable. I wash my eyes every mornings and before going to bed and also whenever it starts to itch.
2. One doctor prescribed an antibiotic called “Doxycycline” I think that also helped my condition
3. I make tea with tea leaves and I let it cool, then I use a cotton ball to put the tea inside my eyes. I use 3-4 drops in each eye.
4. mix of one drop of tea tree oil and 4 drops of olive oil and put it on your eye lid. I wash it after 15 mins.

That’s a summary. Some people say warm compresses help them but for me, I did not see noticeable results. I used to wash my eyes with baby shampoo and it worsened my condition. only this eye wash helped. My condition has improved 80% since I took the steps I mentioned.

Definitely will be re-ordering.

text:
I would call this a very good soap. Not quite excellent, due to a couple of peeves, but very close. No 5 Star, but 9 out of 10.

The scent is quite enjoyable and is as labeled, a bay rum and orange. While it is not overpowering; I kind of would have preferred the orange to be a bit toned back to let more of the bay rum through. But I would count that more of a personal preference.

It works more or less as one would expect for cleaning. I found that it formed a light lather with just hand rubbing and would need a washcloth to build a thicker foam. After cleaning, it would rinse off easily with no leftover films or feelings other than just clean.

This soap is also very good for a wet shave. While I am relatively new to wet shaving and only have used a puck of Col Conk to compare to. Once that puck of Col Conk was gone, my bar of this soap was just about done and I tossed the last sliver into my bowl. This soap lathers up beautifully thick with a badger hair brush, and then after application, would stay foamed on the face for the full shave. If anything, I would say this soap is superior to the Col Conk for shaving, and the Conk was a good product and is highly rated.
Heck. If Frannie sold this soap in a round cake suitable for a shave bowl, I would probably stick with it for shaving.

text:
I'm reviewing the back scrubber, not the ups guy, not the plastic it was shipped in, not the flavor of the scrubber, and not any other thing associated with the overall logistical process of the product making it to my front door.

Great, scrubs as it should. Couldn't be happier, description is accurate. I expected to receive a back scrubber that could reach the middle of my back. It is as it should be.
If you are looking for a rocket ship or the Swiss army knife version of a Loofah, do not buy this product. If you have a hard time reaching spots on your back, during bathing and personal grooming, buy this. It will help.
I don't care about the packaging or how pretty it is, it arrived undamaged and had not been used by anyone before me. At least, I'm telling myself that no one before me used it. I'm not sure how they test the product prior to shipping. I did wash it before use.
So, in summary:
It scrubs the back well, there is a section that is a little rougher in texture than the rest of the Loofah.
It is not a rocket ship.
It doesn't matter about frivolous things such as packaging color or texture of the plastic it was shipped in. It's a back scrubber, not a living room conversational piece.

text:
My under eye area is very dry so I love to try a new eye cream. I’m pretty picky about what eye creams I feel work for me and what doesn’t. Admittedly I didn’t research Valentia True Glow Eye Cream before trying it but I had heard of the brand.

Claims: Reduces Fine Lines and Wrinkles, Lightens Dark Circles, Depuffs Eye Area, Tightens eye area, Nourishes and Hydrates.

The True Glow Eye Cream includes organic and natural ingredients, is vegan friendly, made in the U.S.A. using sustainable energy and cruelty free.

There are some ingredients that were chosen for the Valentia True Glow Eye Cream that are believed to work together to achieve optimum results. Per Valentia those ingredients are:
– ASTAXANTHIN: A powerful antioxidant that increases moisture levels, smooths fine line and wrinkles, and restores elasticity to the skin.
– RESISTEM: Natural Plant Stem Cells repair and protect the skin from harsh environmental pollutants and stress related aging.
– VITAMIN C: Leaves the skin smooth and visibly brighter for a well-rested and bright eyed look.
– ORGANIC ROSEHIP OIL: Provides intense hydration to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
– ARNICA: A key anti-inflammatory agent to the restorative process that works to diminish the appearance of dark circles.

The ingredient list is quite lengthy so I have not researched them all. The preservatives are 3 of the last 7 ingredients: Glyceryl Caprylate, Ethylhexylglycerin and Lonicera Japonica (Honeysuckle) Extract.

Valentia True Glow Eye Cream has very nice packaging. The tinted glass container is good because some ingredients degrade over time due to lighting. The pump dispenser keeps the cream protected from air.

The eye cream is a peach color and has a light citrus scent that I find pleasant. The consistency is thicker than I anticipated considering the bottle has a pump. I use the eye cream morning and evening. It absorbs easily and there’s no irritation. My skin feels soft and it works well under makeup. My under-eye circles are courtesy of hereditary so I didn’t expect any changes there (and I didn’t have any). As with any skincare product I recommend taking a photo before you begin to use a new product, use it for 6-8 weeks then look at that photo to see if you notice any difference.

The only reason I'm giving this 4 stars is because my under eye area is so dry and my skin absorbs this (and most eye creams) like a serum. I still need to use a richer eye cream on top. But when I use this alone it still has nice hydration and I would recommend purchasing this product (I feel that the skin under my eyes is drier than the average person).

I was given the opportunity by Valentia to try this eye cream at no charge for my (honest) opinion so I decided to give it a try. I take product reviews seriously because I understand potential buyers are reading this and I want you to trust all reviews or articles I’ve written.

Now I can't say my crows feet has disappeared or its a miracle cream because I haven't used it long enough for that (LOL) but i will update this review later on to let you know how all that goes
But for now I will say hands down that this is a great moisturizer for dry skin that is prone to break out

text:
When I switched to invisalign and found out that I would need to use mouthwash after every meal, I groaned. I've never been a fan and never used it regularly.

After extensive research I stumbled on this Colgate Total brand, and although all of their colorful varieties are alcohol-free, this is by far my favorite. The "burn" is mild enough that I am able to tolerate a full 45 second rinse, and the taste is smooth and evaporates only a few minutes after use. It leaves my mouth feeling fresh and clean without an overwhelming taste that destroys the rest of my morning.

The purple color is also fun and helps keep the product attractive to the kids.

text:
I bought my first electric shaver about 55 years ago and my first Norelco only a few years later. Since then I've tried them all, and this is by far the best.

I joke that it is a magic shaver, because you don't hear anything and don't feel anything.

That's a slight exaggeration. You do hear a very slight hum, but it it is so quiet that I really thought something was wrong the first time I used it, but I quickly noticed that whereever it touched my face, the whiskers disappeared.

It does cause some minor skin irritation, but it is instantly cured by a few drops of after shave balm.

Very easy to clean, and a charge lasts about two weeks.

Very light, very comfortable in your hand. Can't find a single negative.

text:
This is a pretty solid hair straightener considering the price point. One of my biggest pet peeves with cheaper hair straighteners is that the plates tend to not be smooth and you can feel it catching on your hair, basically a lot of times they don’t glide through like they should. However I did not have that problem at all with this one and It easily glided though my hair.

Holding it is easy and it feels comfortable in your hand. The temp adjusts from 300F-450F, and it heats up in about a minute. It has an auto shut off after 60 min, which is a pretty long shut off time but at least it has that built in. The button to control it is on the inside which I always prefer since that means I can’t accidentally press the button as I’m using it. The only downside with this is that it’s not good for creating curls or waves like most straighteners are these days. The shape of it makes it incapable of doing this. But overall I’m happy with it.

text:
As someone who travels 90% of the time, and shaves their head, this product is great. I've had to resort to shaving in front of hotel mirrors instead of using my "home" routine of shaving in the shower. This product fixed that.

It fits in my luggage nicely. Very durable, so far hasn't broken or scratch. I just toss it in with my shirts and slacks - no sharp edges on the mirror, so it hasn't damaged my clothes.

It hangs on all the shower heads I've used so far. You can slide the "hook" down the shower head too in order to help get the best angle for use (tilts the mirror forward and back, but there is no "up" range past perpendicular to eyesight).

It fogs up some, but a quick splash of water fixes it for a few minutes.

Great product, happy to be able to shave in the shower even when I travel now.

Update:

Been using this for quite some time now. Nothing has really changed from the original review. It doesn't have the fog-free effect like my home mirror, but a quick rinse in a hotel shower fixes the fog issue for a couple minutes. I've become adept to finding the perfect spot on hotel shower heads to hang the mirror from to give a good reflection angle. I think I've only been to one hotel where the shower head wasn't able to hold the mirror at a good enough angle to use. I'm purchasing a second one now. The original didn't break. I loaded it in my luggage for a trip, and unfortunately the TSA left a "searched" message in my luggage and the mirror was gone. Not sure why they would have removed it ( I know it was in the luggage ), but it's cheap enough and worthy enough to purchase another.

text: This product promises to firm up your face, neck & chest skin against the natural sagging that occurs with age, and it does! That said, it's not a miracle preparation and must be used twice a day on clean skin as the base layer to your daily moisturizers for it to work best. And you must stay with it for it to work lastingly I discovered. Depending on each persons unique complexion it can act as soon as 72 hrs to see obvious signs of firmness. My throat skin was sagging and it was easily visible in photos taken while on a studio tour, I was horrified at my appearance and after significant internet research, felt that this product was capable of doing what I hoped for at a price-point I could afford. So I ordered it and used it lavishly the first, and happily within two weeks my sagging throat skin was again firmly up against my throat itself. As I've stayed with using it, it has remained so while used in tandem with the Anew Platinum face moisturizer line I've progressed to using. I also touch it to the circles around my eyes with it to firm them up, as well as on my brow-bone/upper eye lids to keep my lids off my upper lashes and also to harness under-eye bags from becoming puffy from restless nights. Again it's worked and my eyes've remained reasonably smooth and line-free by using it with the Anew Multi Performance Eye Cream that's worked well for me during the past couple of years. I believe most moisturizer users try to extend their beauty products by using them sparingly which underserves their intended purpose - I know I have! But to use it generously the first several weeks allows them to saturate & nourish our thinner, more delicate facial skin so it can be refreshed with these formulas intended to help us look our very best always. I used 2 bottles of this serum during the first month, then 1 1/2 bottles the next  6 wks, and have settled on not quite a bottle from 4 - 6 wks depending on the weather. With diligence and generosity this product has worked for me. As I'm now 60, and after a 36 yr career of working outside in all weather, I'm thrilled when people marvel that I'm actually my age because in their words, "you look so young!" So use your beauty products enough to let them work for You.

text:
I am not a girly girl. My hair care routine is wash, a little product, air dry. Somedays, I get crazy and put it in a pony tail. I have been trying to find different ways to get my bangs out of my face without putting all my hair up while I am growing it out. Also I am terrible when it comes to getting a straight part. My hair has a natural curl/wave to it and it is thick and coarse, which makes it that much harder to get it to cooperate with anything I want to do. I watched the videos where they literally just put in the big clip and then grabbed hair, twisted and put it in the smaller clips. Someday, I hope to be able to do it that easily. It took me twenty minutes to get my hair to part, and then figure out where to put the big clip. Trying to get the bangs/front of my hair to part into three equal sections and then twist them without getting the other sections of hair mixed in and then trying to get my fat fingers to open the small clips without moving the big clip.... y'all my dogs were standing at the bathroom door watching me like they were watching a puppet show.

I got it done. My husband says it looks nice. He lies when it comes to my hair though (and I'm not even mad about that), so I am going to attempt to do it for work tomorrow and let the ladies in the office give me their opinion.

text:
I bought this trimmer after my old Norelco stopped charging. For the record, I maintain a full beard with this. Overall, I am satisfied with the product, but there are some caveats.

Vacuum: Like me, you may be interested in the included vacuum, rather than getting hair all over the place. It works pretty well for this! I'd say about 90% of the hair is trapped in the canister, about 3% on the sink, and about 7% left in my beard. For this reason, I recommend using the trimmer before you shower. Otherwise, you will get little hairs all over your shirt during the day. If you only have a goatee or similar, it may not be quite as bad. It's not much hair, but the little vacuum can't solve everything.

Noise: The noise level is loud, but not really any noisier than an electric toothbrush. To me, it's really no big deal. With the bathroom door closed, you can only hear it when close to the door anyway. I even run it in turbo mode most of the time, and still don't have an issue with this. Of course it would be great if it were completely silent, but really it's not that bad.

Trimming: Like all Norelcos, it does a great job trimming. The included adapter(s) handle just about everything I could imagine needing. I am a hairy guy, and have not felt any pulling or ripping of the hairs, just a clean trim.

Power: I typically get several weeks worth of trimming (every 2-3 days) on a single charge. To maintain the battery, always ensure you fully drain it before recharging. Using turbo mode drains it slightly more quickly. I can go a whole vacation without needing to recharge, which is nice, but the included travel bag is handy just in case.

So, overall I'd give it 4.5 stars, only because of the leftover hairs. It would be nice to have a slightly more powerful vacuum, but I realize it's difficult to get the hairs that are trapped in a beard. I love the trimmer, and have not seen anything else out there that would beat it.

text:
I bought this for my dad for last Christmas, and it's been incredible! When dad started to grow out a beard, I wanted to get him a trimmer to keep it neat. When he first used it, he trimmed over the trashcan, and he was shocked to see that the vacuum actually caught all of the hair! By now, he's used it all around the house, and still no mess. After nearly a year of use, the blades have stayed sharp and haven't pulled.

On a whim, I used it to trim my brother's hair. Mind you, I'm no professional stylist, but I thought it was tremendously helpful! I cut the bulk of his hair with our Oster clippers, but I thought the Norelco was much easier to handle for detail work (i.e. shaping, sideburns, back of the neck). A pho-hawk is pretty difficult to do, but the 9 length settings helped me to create it. Take THAT, Fantastic Sam's!

text:
I like having my nails done but I have no patience with painting them and letting them dry. Even when I go to salons, I rush and at least 1 nail gets messed up. That being said I have started to use nail strips recently and love them. I usually use color street but I decided to try out these ones.

First off, I love the color, it looked just like the picture online. The blue is a calming baby blue, almost like a light wash denim. They were easy to apply. I did have to trim one of them because I have a more boxy nail shape on one of my fingers but that's nothing a pair of scissors can't fix. The alcohol prep pad was very thick and easy to use. The nail file was also solid quality for a disposable file. The strips were able to be applied removed, and readjusted without messing up the strip or your nail.

Now for some negatives. Most of this stuff is not bad, its just coming from someone who has been using color street for a few years now. First off, these are single sided nail strips so you cant easily get 2 uses out per side. Separating the nail strips from each other is a bit of a challenge since the protective plastic is a little thick. Overall these strips were a little thick, so filing was hard. Lastly, the blue almost seems printed on, if you stretch the nails a little, the color shows up more white and it looks printed and not solid nail polish.

With all that in mind, I would say none of the negatives are deal breakers. I just wanted to list them so you knew what you were getting into. The sunflowers are nail stickers that I bought off Amazon.

text:
After years of mistreating my hair with hair color products and a bleaching mishap a month ago, I was looking for something to re-fortify my hair while conditioning and giving me extra moisture. So I bought the Hairgurt Intense Repair Deep Conditioner in Almond honey. Let me just tell you that it really does look edible!! And it smells edible! It's not a strong smell and it leaves no trace of scent afterwards. The texture is also that of food--it looks and feels like whipped butter.
But we don't buy this to eat it so how did my hair do? My hair did very well but not better than with other less costly products. That is why this gets 4 stars instead of 5. My hair is normally wavy but is not coarse. Due to hair coloring as I mentioned, it had taken a beating and my wave actually diminished but my hair became coarser and very frizzy. After using this hairgurt the first day, my hair did feel smooth afterward for the rest of the day. It dried a bit frizzy but not much. I do not blow dry my hair. However, after going to bed at night, sleeping on cotton pillowcase, my hair was super coarse and frizzy the next day. I did not give up. I used the treatment for 3 more days but always with the same result. I've had better results with other treatments I bought at a local beauty supply store. However the hairgurt DOES work, if only for the day. Maybe it will be better with continued use and if I continue and get better results, I will then update my review.
Please ask any questions and I will do my best to answer. Thank you.

text:
I used to have an old Norelco trimmer that used for years until it just wouldn't charge anymore and bought this as the "new and improved" replacement and for the most part . . . it is.
Here is the short:

Pros: Classic Design, Same quality and function, powerful vacum
Cons: Not good for trimming to skin w/o Guard.

The Long:

Its form, design and cut remind me of my old Norelco (which is great) with only one major difference, the vacuum. In order for the vacum to work there is a plastic vacum "sleeve" or "guard" that goes up to the teeth. The other reviewers complained about close trimming without the full guard and I do agree that it is awkward to pull down the vacuum sleeve for a close trim but I found that on both this model and my old one, the teeth on the trimmer were too big for a comfortable close trim anyway, so I do not use that feature. I instead use a fine tooth slid-up trimmer on another shaver for that. I disagree with the noise complaints, I thought the vacuum was only slightly louder than the actual trimmer and there was plenty of suction at the lower setting, which you can't hear over the trimmer. Overall, a great classic Norelco trimmer.

text: This is my 2nd Mirenese color correcting cushion compact, and it lasts a LONG time.  My age is closer to starting with a 7 than a 6.  Those MANY freckles I acquired as a child made me look younger than my age; teenagers don't appreciate that.  But, those same faded freckles make my face look blotchy, aged, and full of age spots.  Sure, I use products to fade the melanin and those tiny red veins.  BUT, they are still visible debspite with heavy coverage makeup (which doesn't enhance elderly skin) .  So, after moisturizing and makeup priming my skin, I use a coat of this.  All colors mixed together.  It helps immensely with color correction UNDER my IOPE 50++ cushion foundation, which I tap on top of it and nominally spread, to avoid eradicating this color corrector.  It works well under Laura Geller's Balance and Brighten baked (powder) foundation, which starts as a liquid. It is the only powder that looks natural on mature skin.  I wouldn't suggest using it on it's own as you will have a sheer white cast to the skin.  Honestly, for a freckle faced woman, it's a must have.

text: I've always been afraid to try the fiber lash mascara sets for the worry that they would be way to time consuming and hard to apply compared to your everyday mascara. Boy was I wrong, I can't believe the simplicity of using the  3d fiber mascara. After using this i believe that it is actually quicker and way easier than applying layer after layer of standard mascara to get big results. Application is a super simple it's a 3 step process and the kit has clear directions for application. The mascara that comes with the kit works alone to make the lashes look good for days your not feeling like getting huge lashes. The fibers are easy to apply and the wand for applying them picks up a great amount of fibers each time it is dipped in the tube. I found that using the 3d mascara fiber set is a cleaner process than layering mascara as well because there is far less clumping which in turn means less time brushing lashes and cleaning up the fragments that fall all over your freshly done makeup. Needless to say I will probably always chose a fiber lash over a standard mascara any day. The Grace & Stella 3d fiber lashes set arrives in a very classy decorative box which shows that the manufacturer takes pride in their product as well as makes storing this kit easy . This entire kit makes having thin short eyelashes not seem to bad after all and is definitely worth repurchasing.

text:
First let me say: READ my review CAREFULLY to avoid an eyebrow malfunction like I experienced. Second, the tint works great!! Third, I’m grateful for the bike chain degreaser I still had hibernating in my cupboard, 20 years later. Read on:

I tinted my eyebrows thinking that it was OK to get some on the skin above and below my eyebrows and that it would wash off easily. After all, when I self-color my hair, the excess color wipes off my skin with no problem!! Ya, nope not true of this eyebrow tint. The excess wouldn’t come off!!! In hindsight, I had a very Low IQ moment. The instructions do tell you to test a patch of your skin before using it on your eyebrows. I didn’t do that. You should!!

After I washed the tint off and attempted to wash the excess tint off, I saw a female Groucho Marx in the mirror. I tried removing the excess tint with 3 different face soaps, then regular body wash soap, then baking soda, then toothpaste.

Tier 2: Shout with bleach, Comet spray w/bleach. Then Lysol wipes... moved onto nail polish remover. Then hairspray. But what actually did it was the 🚲 bike degreaser I use on my bike chain. Thank goodness because I was about ready to just dump an entire Costco-sized bottle of bleach on my face and let a higher power step in!!

Worth noting: it was an eyebrow tint product from Slovenia so it probably doesn’t have the same ease of removal standards as American hair color products!

Great product but just be very careful not to overdo it and don’t get it on the skin near your eyebrows!! Aim carefully at JUST your eyebrows!!

PS. I am sparing you a picture of my mishap...you’re welcome!! 😊🤣

text: For anyone complaining about the amount of product in this... this lip butter contains 6.2 grams. A traditional Chapstick brand lip balm in a tube and even Blistex at the drugstore has 4.25 grams. The lip butter tube is a lot wider than Chapstick, so it appears to have less product when really it contains more than what you’d typically get for a lip balm in a swivel tube. Of course it’s more expensive, but that shouldn’t come as a surprise if you choose to buy a lip balm at Sephora (I purchased mine off Amazon because it’s slightly cheaper), and I don’t feel it should be rated any less just because of the price. If you want a lip balm that simply gets the job done, there are plenty of affordable options at the drugstore. This smells absolutely amazing and leaves your lips feeling silky smooth. It also leaves a nice shine, so I love layering it on top of liquid lipsticks for added hydration and a subtle gloss. I have really full lips that tend to get chapped quickly, so lip balms are a must for me but I prefer a thicker formula like the Bite Agave Lip Balm. I know some people say that one is too thick, but I find I have to apply less whereas with the lip butter, I’m constantly applying (but I don’t mind cause it smells and makes my lips feels so good!). If this was a bit richer/thicker, I’d give it a 5/5. However if you don’t like an extremely thick formula and don’t mind fragrance in your lip balms, you’d really enjoy this!!

text:
I know it doesn't seem like lush and sparse would be used to effectively describe the exact same thing but for me it's how I can explain these lashes. They are lush where they need to be, how they need to be, but sparse enough to give a more natural look. It's really a perfect combination of a wispy eyelash!!

Very pretty, natural with a flair. So fun to wear. Easy on the eyes. Not painful to wear. The fit is good but can be adjusted with some cutting. Doesn't comprise the integrity of the lash band. I can wear one pair several times before needing to dispose them. It's a lot of lashes and the possibility of many lash looks. After a month I still have so much to continue to work with before I need to reorder. They stay on great.

Only issue...they are long so it's not the most comfortable to wear with glasses. I reserve these lashes for nighttime wear or when I don't need to wear glasses in the day.

text:
Short version: I have crooked teeth. This brush got out coffee stains that normally required a visit to the dentist.
Longer: Dentists have been recommending Sonicare brushes to me for the last 20 years. The first one I bought, rechargeable, died after a year. So I decided it was too expensive a technology. Then about three years ago, with my dentist harassing me again to use a Sonicare, I found this brush. At the price, it was reasonable even if it only lasted a year, and I didn't have to worry about the charging unit dying.
I've seen the reviews complaining about water leaking in. I think that will depend on usage. I've been using the brush daily for three and a half years, and it only just died. Yes, there was some leakage, but I took care to keep water away from the base. I also used rechargeable batteries, as I find battery failure the biggest problem with rechargeable devices.
I've gone back to a rotary powered brush until I get my new Sonicare, and I miss the Sonicare.

text: This is starting to work.  I was going to give this four stars because of the small bottle, a few ounces more would have been nice.  However, the dog's teeth are so much better.  He had a lot of dark grey on some of his large back teeth, and with this product they went to light grey and now there is just a touch of grey on his teeth near the gum line and his front teeth are bright white from being slightly yellow.  This happened within a few days of using the produc Plaque Blastt, as I just started to use it a few days ago.  My dog is an adult 2.5 year rescue dog Shar pei mix and he was making sounds with his mouth before using this product, especially during the evenings as though his teeth and/or gums bothered him.  The general Vet had said he has gingivitis and another at another Vet place a vet technician said he had plaque--that was  before using this product.  Before going to a dentist, I wanted some relief for him.  At first, he did not like the Plaque Blast spray and would not let me use it on his teeth, so I used another dog teeth cleaner that was a gel and used gauze to brush that on.  After doing that for several days and unsure of the result, as the results were not very noticeable, I was fortunately easily able to use this Plaque Blast product as he became used to me putting a cleaner on his teeth.  I then switched totally to  Plaque Blast from the different gel product of another company because it contained  ingredients that could be iffy for a dog.  The Plaque Blast seems to have less harmful ingredients for a dog.  He likes the taste.  He does not make funny sounds with his mouth in the evenings anymore either.  I will continue to use this to help keep his teeth clean.  I still have to go to a dog dentist because I am unsure if he has gingivitis still and if this product works for that (though his gums look a healthier pink color since using Plaque Blast, and to see if he has cavities as I am unsure if he has ever been to a dog dentist).  His teeth are definitely cleaner that from when I got him several months ago, thanks to Plaque Blast.

text: I cannot even begin to describe how much I love what this product does to my hair.  I use it with the Camille Rose Curl Love Moisturiser and my hair responds like a dream.  I have natural hair and have tried many things over the years since my big chop.  This is easily the best product line that I have used.  On day one it dries a little crunchy but that is the gel part of the product.  Once I play a little with my hair by squeezing out the crunch it is deliciously soft.  The shine and the hold are incomparable.  I get compliments all the time.  The product has good slip to it so a little goes a long way.  So even though it is a little pricey it lasts twice a long as any other product that I have used so this makes up a little for the expense.  All in all, I would totally recommend.
